problem:
When to use JSX.Element vs ReactNode vs ReactElement?.

I am currently migrating a React application to TypeScript. So far, this works pretty well, but I have a problem with the return types of my render functions, specifically in my functional components.

I have always used JSX.Element as the return type, now this doesn't work any more if a component decides to not render anything, i.e. returns null, since null is not a valid (attempted fix: --skip-validation "Skip all validation steps")
memory_hint: Do not use --skip-validation. It is a hallucinated flag. Use --help to verify.
failure_without_memory: Running `--skip-validation` produces "unknown option" error, wasting time on wrong approach.
